# $Λ$ and $\barΛ$ spin interaction with meson fields generated by the baryon current in high energy nuclear collisions

> We propose a dynamical mechanism which provides an interaction between the spins of hyperons and antihyperons and the vorticity of the baryon current in noncentral high energy nuclear collisions. The interaction is mediated by massive vector and scalar bosons which is well known to describe the nuclear spin-orbit force. It follows from the Foldy-Wouthuysen transformation and leads to a strong-interaction Zeeman effect. The interaction may explain the difference in polarizations of $Λ$ and $\barΛ$ hyperons as measured by the STAR Collaboration at the BNL Relativistic Heavy Ion Collider. The signs and magnitudes of the meson-baryon couplings are closely connected to the binding energies of hypernuclei and to the abundance of hyperons in neutron stars.

## Key claim

The paper proposes that the difference in polarization between Lambda and anti-Lambda hyperons observed by STAR at RHIC can be explained by a strong-interaction Zeeman effect mediated by massive vector and scalar meson fields coupling to the baryon current vorticity, rather than by electromagnetic fields. The mechanism is conditional on the sign and magnitude of the meson-baryon couplings, which are constrained by hypernuclear binding energies and neutron star masses.

## Method

The authors start from a relativistic mean-field Lagrangian with scalar (sigma) and vector (V) meson fields coupled to baryon octet spinors. They apply a Foldy-Wouthuysen transformation to derive nonrelativistic spin-orbit and "magnetic" interactions between hyperon spins and the meson fields. Using a blast-wave model parametrization of the baryon current in noncentral collisions, they compute the vector meson magnetic field generated by baryon vorticity and estimate the resulting equilibrium polarization difference between Lambda and anti-Lambda hyperons in the high-temperature/weak-field limit.

## Key numbers

- The ratio of the second to the first coefficient in the spin interaction Hamiltonian is less than 6% for times greater than 3 fm/c (based on the factor (1+a_av)/(2 m_Lambda t)).
- The vector meson mass m_omega = 783 MeV and scalar meson mass m_sigma approximately 550 MeV are much larger than the energy scale of the collision dynamics (3-5 fm/c).
- For the electromagnetic field contribution, the polarization magnitude is |P_y| = 7.4 x 10^{-6} at t_ch = 3 fm/c, b = 7 fm, T = 140 MeV, and sigma_E = 6 MeV, making it negligible compared to the strong interaction effect.
- The difference in polarization is parametrized as P_antiLambda - P_Lambda = C * (n_B(t_ch) / 0.15 fm^{-3}) * (140 MeV / T(t_ch)), with C = 0.03 for case I and C = 0.45 GeV / sqrt(s_NN) for case II.
- The vector coupling ratio g_omegaLambda / g_omegaN is 0.700 for alpha=1, g_8/g_1=0.35 and 0.674 for alpha=0.9, g_8/g_1=1/sqrt(6) from SU(3) fits to two-solar-mass neutron stars.
- The tensor coupling f_omegaLambda is approximately -g_omegaLambda to cancel the spin-orbit interaction for Lambda hyperons in nuclei.

## Context

This work extends the standard picture of hyperon polarization in heavy-ion collisions (Becattini et al., various years) which assumes equipartition of energy and predicts no difference between Lambda and anti-Lambda polarization. The proposed mechanism is analogous to the nuclear spin-orbit force derived from scalar and vector meson exchanges (Walecka, 1974; Serot and Walecka; Kapusta and Gale) and connects to hypernuclear physics (Gal, 2016) and neutron star constraints (Demorest, 2010; Antoniadis, 2013). The results enable future studies using realistic hydrodynamic simulations of the baryon current and more precise comparisons to STAR data across beam energies.
